text.title: risk-register Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for risk-register. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every risk must have exactly one named individual as owner; "the team" is not valid.
rule.rationale: Risks without a single owner are observed, not managed. Accountability requires a name.

rule.statement: Every trigger must be an observable signal — a metric, log event, or date — not a subjective phrase like "if it gets bad".
rule.rationale: Unobservable triggers cannot be monitored; risks activate without warning.

rule.statement: Every Accept strategy entry must include a contingency_amount or contingency_plan field; Accept without contingency is invalid.
rule.rationale: Accept without a contingency is denial dressed as a strategy. When the risk materialises there is no prepared response.

rule.statement: Risk score must equal probability_level x impact_level; stored score must match computed value or the register is invalid.
rule.rationale: Score integrity is required for heatmap accuracy and prioritisation correctness.

rule.statement: Include at least one opportunity (positive risk) for every ten threats; reject registers that contain only threats.
rule.rationale: Sponsor approval rates rise when upside is made visible. LLMs drop opportunities by default.

rule.statement: Calibrate the impact scale with a project-specific reference point (e.g. "High impact = greater than $50k or greater than 1 month delay") before scoring.
rule.rationale: Uncalibrated scales produce inconsistent scores across assessors and over time.

rule.statement: Risk-driven contingency (sum of probability x cost_impact per open risk) must replace flat-percentage reserves for any project with an active risk register.
rule.rationale: Flat reserves systematically over- or under-fund based on risk profile; risk-driven calculation calibrates to actual exposure.
